extracted.brandBackground: Goldfish Swim School provides specialized quality swim instruction and aquatic activities to children aged 4 months to 12 years.

leadership.name: Chris McCuiston
leadership.background: Co-founded the brand and has served as CEO since formation in June 2008.
leadership.whyItMatters: Provides long-term executive leadership and continuity since inception.

leadership.name: Andrew McCuiston
leadership.background: Served as VP of Franchise Development from 2008 to 2018, and President since May 2018.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees the strategic growth and operational direction of the franchise system.

leadership.name: Jennifer McCuiston
leadership.background: Thought leader designing and executing the swim curriculum since October 2005.
leadership.whyItMatters: Ensures the core educational product and curriculum remain high quality.

leadership.name: Matthew Davis
leadership.background: Served as CFO since February 2022, previously VP of Finance and Director of Finance.
leadership.whyItMatters: Manages the financial health and reporting of the franchisor entity.

leadership.name: Daniel Israel
leadership.role: Chief Franchising Officer and General Counsel
leadership.background: Served as Chief Franchising Officer and General Counsel since February 2022.
leadership.whyItMatters: Directs legal compliance and franchise sales operations.

leadership.name: Michael Skitzki
leadership.background: Served as COO since February 2022, previously VP of Operations and COO of Franworth.
leadership.whyItMatters: Responsible for day-to-day operational support and system execution.

hiddenCosts.name: Technology Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Currently $700 to $1,295 per month depending on service level selected.

hiddenCosts.name: Transfer Fee
hiddenCosts.description: 50% of the then-current initial franchise fee if transferee is new to the system.

hiddenCosts.name: Audit Expenses
hiddenCosts.description: Approximately $1,500 to $5,000 plus interest on underpayments if audit reveals underreporting of 3% or more.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: Disputes must be resolved by arbitration or litigation only in Michigan, which may increase costs.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Payments
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must pay minimum royalty and marketing fees regardless of sales levels.

extracted.conceptRationale: The concept is an indoor swim school offering lessons and aquatic activities, which is an experiential child-focused service.
extracted.staffingRationale: Requires swim instructors, lifeguards, reception staff, and a designated manager to operate safely.

riskReasons: Capital gap of $2,454,998 is ~10x your liquid — extreme leverage; the deal rests almost entirely on outside capital.
A high-severity operational tripwire is disclosed — see the tripwires section.

midCohort.label: All Franchised Schools Open > 12 Months - Average Total Revenue

source.label: All Franchised Schools Open > 12 Months - Average Total Revenue

notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.

underwriting.assessment: With $250,000 liquid against a $2,704,998 mid-point build-out, there is a $2,454,998 capital gap. An SBA loan or an equity partner is required to do this deal. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $131,593/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: The concept is an indoor swim school offering lessons and aquatic activities, which is an experiential child-focused service.

benchmark.label: Experiential / entertainment venue
benchmark.dominantRisk: Fixed-cost coverage and utilization — big-box rent and equipment R&M against variable foot traffic. Slow weeks hurt more than COGS.
benchmark.rampNote: 6–18 months to a mature traffic/booking base; year-one runs well under the Item 19 average.
considerations: COGS is modest; the real costs are labor (~22–30%), big-footprint rent, and equipment maintenance.
Ask about utilization/occupancy rates and seasonality at Item 20 units — the model lives or dies on weekday/off-peak traffic.
Confirm equipment R&M and replacement reserves; experiential gear wears and isn't in the pro forma.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 12–22% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

buildup.label: − Occupancy / rent (not disclosed)
buildup.note: Item 7 disclosed no rent figure — benchmark occupancy applied so the line above isn't overstated

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: conventionally staffed · ≈ 10.5–14.4 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Experiential / entertainment venue” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Item 7 disclosed no rent — occupancy estimated at the 10–18% category band.
Estimated at the 8–18% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 22–30% conventionally staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: We couldn't extract enough of this franchisor's financial statements to assess its condition. Request the audited Item 21 financials and have them reviewed before committing.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Derived from the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
